task_completion.description: The original landscape image is opened in the image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image editor (e.g., GIMP) is open with a landscape image visible in the main workspace.

task_completion.description: The Canvas Size dialog box is opened to modify the image dimensions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Canvas Size' (or similar) is visible on the screen over the image editor.

task_completion.description: The width and height values in the Canvas Size dialog are increased by approximately 100 pixels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Canvas Size dialog shows new width and height values that are roughly 100 pixels larger than the original dimensions (e.g., width changing from 800 to ~900).

task_completion.description: The offset is adjusted to center the original image within the new canvas dimensions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Canvas Size dialog, the preview shows the image centered, or the offset X and Y values are set to approximately half of the expansion size (e.g., ~50px each), or a 'Center' button is clicked.

task_completion.description: The canvas is successfully expanded with the original image centered and a clean background fill around it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main image editor workspace shows the landscape image surrounded by an even border (approx 50px on all sides) of background color or transparency, indicating a 100px total expansion in both dimensions.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the image editor rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP menus, dialogs, and buttons, rather than a terminal window executing image processing commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with an actual image editing software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard GIMP interface (toolbars, dockable dialogs, menus) is visible, not a mock-up, text editor, or script.

integrity.description: The agent performed the steps manually rather than just opening a pre-modified image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the original image, the dialog box being manipulated, and the final result, rather than just the final expanded image appearing instantly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original images are landscape photos (800x5xx pixels). The target canvas size should be approximately 900x6xx pixels (±20 pixel tolerance). The original image must remain perfectly centered within the new canvas, resulting in an even border of roughly 50 pixels on all sides. The background fill should be clean (either transparent or a solid color).
